---
title: "From pixels to patterns — results and visualisation"
author: "Emma Belaud"
format:
  html:
    css: css/styles.css
    embed-resources: true
    code-tools: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
---

# Overview

## Role in the pipeline

This is the **final step** of the SEM pipeline, following:

1.  `data_preparation.qmd` — assembles the SEM database
2.  `sem_diagnostic.qmd` — selects window duration, transformation, and valid windows
3.  `3_SEM_modelisation.qmd` — fits all models and writes result databases

**No modelling is performed here.** Every number in every figure comes directly from pre-computed CSV files. Re-running this script is fast and safe — it never touches a model.

## Input databases

| File | What it contains |
|----|----|
| `H1_adjusted_values.csv` | Per-observation null-model residuals with date, land-use label, season |
| `H1_model_summary.csv` | β, SE, t, p, Hedge's g, R² from the two-step H1 model |
| `DAG_coefficients.csv` | Path coefficients from the full-dataset piecewise SEM |
| `DAG_model_fit.csv` | Fisher's C, AIC for the full-dataset piecewise SEM |
| `SEM_results_database.csv` | Rolling-window path coefficients for all taxa |
| `indirect_effects.csv` | Rolling-window indirect effect products for all taxa |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r setup}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SETUP
# Loads packages, reads shared metadata (microclimate variable names), and
# defines all visual constants and helper functions used across sections 1–3.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

invisible(Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "C"))   # force English month names in plots
dir.create("../output", showWarnings = FALSE)

libs <- c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "patchwork", "kableExtra", "colorspace", "nlme")
invisible(lapply(libs, require, character.only = TRUE))


# ── Microclimate variable names ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# Written by data_preparation.qmd so that axis labels automatically reflect
# the actual column names in the dataset (e.g. "soil_temp", "VWC").
microclimate_index <- readLines("../output/microclimate_index.txt")
mc1_pred <- microclimate_index[1]
mc2_pred <- microclimate_index[2]


# ── Land-use colour palette ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Dark green = treed plots (A), burnt orange = cultivated plots (C).
pos_colours <- c("Treed (A)" = "#3d5a3e", "Cultivated (C)" = "#c1603a")


# ── Causal pathway metadata ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# One row per pathway.
#   path_id  — machine key, join key in all downstream code
#   label    — human-readable string for legends and axes
#   colour   — warm tones for root-mediated paths, cool for microclimate
#   ltype    — solid = direct/composite | dashed = two-step | dotted = three-step
path_meta <- tribble(
  ~path_id,         ~label,                                                ~colour,   ~ltype,
  "direct",         "Direct (lu\u2192fauna)",                              "#DCB879", "solid",
  "via_mc1",        paste0("lu\u2192", mc1_pred, "\u2192fauna"),           "#c2cab2", "dashed",
  "via_mc2",        paste0("lu\u2192", mc2_pred, "\u2192fauna"),           "#555d45", "dashed",
  "via_root",       "lu\u2192root\u2192fauna",                             "#955f42", "dashed",
  "via_mc1_root",   paste0("lu\u2192", mc1_pred, "\u2192root\u2192fauna"), "#dea88b", "dashed",
  "via_mc2_root",   paste0("lu\u2192", mc2_pred, "\u2192root\u2192fauna"), "#d1855c", "dashed",
  "total_indirect", "Total indirect",                                       "#999999", "solid",
  "total_effect",   "Total effect",                                         "#444444", "solid"
)

# Named vectors for ggplot scale_*_manual() calls
path_colours <- setNames(path_meta$colour, path_meta$path_id)
path_ltypes  <- setNames(path_meta$ltype,  path_meta$path_id)
path_labels  <- setNames(path_meta$label,  path_meta$path_id)


# ── Season background bands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Spring = Mar–May | Summer = Jun–Aug | Autumn = Sep–Nov | Winter = Dec–Feb
season_order <- c("Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter")
season_fills <- c(Spring = "#f9f9f9", Summer = "#eeeeee",
                  Autumn = "#f9f9f9", Winter = "#eeeeee")


# ── Agricultural intervention landmarks ───────────────────────────────────────
interventions <- data.frame(
  date  = as.Date(c("2024-07-08", "2024-07-15",
                    "2024-11-22", "2024-11-25", "2025-03-14")),
  event = c("Wheat Harvest", "Sorghum Sown",
            "Sorghum Mown",  "Tilled",       "Chickpea Sown")
)


#' Build season annotation layers for a ggplot time-series
#'
#' @param date_range  Length-2 Date vector matching the plot x-axis.
#' @param y_label_pos Vertical position of season labels; Inf = top of panel.
#' @param label_size  Font size (ggplot units) for season labels.
#' @return A list of ggplot annotation/geom layers.
make_season_layers <- function(date_range, y_label_pos = Inf, label_size = 2.2) {
  bands <- tibble(
    season  = names(season_fills),
    m_start = c(3L, 6L, 9L, 12L),
    m_end   = c(6L, 9L, 12L,  3L),
    fill    = unname(season_fills)
  ) %>%
    crossing(year = seq(year(date_range[1]), year(date_range[2]))) %>%
    mutate(
      xmin = as.Date(paste(year, m_start, "01", sep = "-")),
      xmax = as.Date(paste(if_else(m_end < m_start, year + 1L, year),
                           m_end, "01", sep = "-")),
      xmid = xmin + (xmax - xmin) / 2
    ) %>%
    filter(xmin < date_range[2], xmax > date_range[1]) %>%
    mutate(xmin = pmax(xmin, date_range[1]),
           xmax = pmin(xmax, date_range[2]))

  rect_layers <- pmap(
    bands %>% select(xmin, xmax, fill),
    \(xmin, xmax, fill) annotate(
      "rect", xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
      fill = fill, alpha = 1
    )
  )
  label_layer <- geom_text(
    data = bands,
    aes(x = xmid, y = y_label_pos, label = season),
    inherit.aes = FALSE, vjust = 1.6, size = label_size,
    color = "grey60", fontface = "italic", family = "sans"
  )
  c(rect_layers, list(label_layer))
}


#' Base ggplot theme for all SEM figures
#'
#' @param base_size  Base font size in pt (default 8).
#' @param legend_pos Passed to legend.position.
theme_sem <- function(base_size = 8, legend_pos = "none") {
  theme_classic(base_size = base_size) %+replace%
    theme(
      panel.border      = element_rect(color = "grey20", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.3),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey94", linewidth = 0.25),
      panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
      panel.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      axis.line         = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks        = element_line(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.3),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(2, "pt"),
      axis.title        = element_text(size = base_size - 0.5, color = "grey20"),
      axis.text         = element_text(size = base_size - 1.5, color = "grey30"),
      strip.text        = element_text(size = base_size - 1, face = "bold",
                                       color = "grey15"),
      strip.background  = element_rect(fill = "grey96", color = "grey96",
                                       linewidth = 0.3),
      plot.title        = element_text(size = base_size + 0.5, face = "bold",
                                       hjust = 0, color = "grey10",
                                       margin = margin(b = 3)),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = base_size - 1, color = "grey45",
                                       hjust = 0, margin = margin(b = 5)),
      plot.caption      = element_text(size = base_size - 2, color = "grey55",
                                       hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 4)),
      legend.position   = legend_pos,
      legend.background = element_blank(),
      legend.key        = element_blank(),
      legend.title      = element_text(size = base_size - 1, face = "bold",
                                       color = "grey20"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = base_size - 1.5, color = "grey30"),
      legend.key.size   = unit(8, "pt"),
      legend.spacing.x  = unit(3, "pt"),
      plot.margin       = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)
    )
}


# ── Load pre-computed result databases ────────────────────────────────────────
# All modelling was performed in 3_SEM_modelisation.qmd.

h1_adj <- read.csv("../output/H1_adjusted_values.csv",
                   na.strings = c("", "NA")) %>%
  mutate(
    position_label = factor(position_label,
                            levels = c("Treed (A)", "Cultivated (C)")),
    season         = factor(season, levels = season_order)
  )

h1_summary <- read.csv("../output/H1_model_summary.csv",
                       na.strings = c("", "NA"))

dag_table <- read.csv("../output/DAG_coefficients.csv",
                      na.strings = c("", "NA"))

dag_fit   <- read.csv("../output/DAG_model_fit.csv",
                      na.strings = c("", "NA"))

sem_results <- read.csv("../output/SEM_results_database.csv",
                        na.strings = c("", "NA")) %>%
  mutate(season = factor(season, levels = season_order))

indirect_effects <- read.csv("../output/indirect_effects.csv",
                             na.strings = c("", "NA"))

# Faunal transformation used during modelling (e.g. "log", "sqrt_plus_one").
transfo_fauna <- h1_summary$transformation[1]
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. H1 — Direct effect of land-use type on faunal activity

> **Land-use type directly structures faunal activity, with treed habitats sustaining lower but more temporally stable activity than cultivated zones.**

### How the values are computed (two-step approach)

Figures in this section show **position-adjusted faunal activity** — null-model residuals shifted to the absolute scale by adding the Step-1 intercept.

**Step 1 — Remove spatial structure.** A mixed model with only an intercept is fitted on the full dataset. Random effects for orientation and depth absorb all variation attributable to scanner placement:

$$y^* \sim 1 + (1 \mid \text{orientation} / \text{depth}) + \text{AR}(1)$$

**Step 2 — Test the land-use effect on residuals.**

$$\hat{\varepsilon}_{\text{Step 1}} \sim \text{land\_use}$$

β = mean(residuals at C) − mean(residuals at A).

```{r fig-h1-combined}
#| label: fig-h1-combined
#| fig-cap: >
#|   **Direct effect of land-use type on faunal activity (H1).**
#|   (A) Distribution of position-adjusted faunal activity pooled across the
#|   monitoring period. (B) Temporal fluctuations with LOESS smoothers
#|   (span = 0.18). Green = treed (A); orange = cultivated (C). Shaded bands =
#|   meteorological seasons. Dotted red lines = agricultural interventions.
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

# ── Back-transform residuals to absolute scale ────────────────────────────────
# Adding the Step-1 intercept converts null-model residuals into
# position-adjusted faunal activity values interpretable on the response scale.
h1_intercept <- h1_summary$estimate[h1_summary$predictor == "(Intercept)" &
                                      h1_summary$step == 1]
if (length(h1_intercept) == 0 || is.na(h1_intercept)) {
  h1_intercept <- mean(h1_adj$adj_value, na.rm = TRUE)
  message("H1 intercept not found — using empirical mean as fallback.")
}

h1_adj <- h1_adj %>%
  mutate(abs_value = adj_value + h1_intercept)

# ── Shared y-axis limits (rounded to 0.1) ────────────────────────────────────
shared_ylim <- c(
  floor(  min(h1_adj$abs_value, na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10,
  ceiling(max(h1_adj$abs_value, na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10
)

# ── Panel A: boxplot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Right border suppressed so panels merge visually at their shared edge.
p_box <- ggplot(
  h1_adj,
  aes(x = position_label, y = abs_value,
      fill = position_label, colour = position_label)
) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.05, size = 0.3, shape = 20) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outliers = FALSE,
               linewidth = 0.8, fatten = 1.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pos_colours) +
  scale_fill_manual(values   = pos_colours) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Treed", "Cultivated")) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = paste0("Faunal activity\n(position-adjusted, ",
                  transfo_fauna, " scale)")) +
  guides(fill = "none", colour = "none") +
  theme_sem() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 6.5, face = "bold"),
    panel.border     = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y.left = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x      = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.margin      = margin(t = 5, r = 0, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Panel B: LOESS time series ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Left border and y-axis suppressed — carried by panel A.
p_temp <- ggplot(
  h1_adj %>% mutate(date = as.Date(date)),
  aes(x = date, colour = position_label, fill = position_label)
) +
  make_season_layers(range(as.Date(h1_adj$date), na.rm = TRUE),
                     y_label_pos = shared_ylim[2]) +
  geom_vline(data = interventions, aes(xintercept = date),
             colour = "darkred", linetype = "dotted",
             alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(y = abs_value), size = 0.5, alpha = 0.15, shape = 16) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = abs_value), method = "loess", formula = y ~ x,
              span = 0.18, linewidth = 0.7, se = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pos_colours, name = "Land-use type",
                      labels = c("Treed (A)", "Cultivated (C)")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values   = pos_colours, name = "Land-use type",
                    labels   = c("Treed (A)", "Cultivated (C)")) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "3 months",
               expand = expansion(mult = 0.01)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  guides(fill = "none", colour = "none") +
  theme_sem(legend_pos = "bottom") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 6.5),
    legend.direction  = "horizontal",
    axis.title.y      = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y      = element_blank(),
    panel.border      = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y.right = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.margin       = margin(t = 5, r = 5, b = 5, l = 0, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Assemble ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
h1_combined <- (p_box + p_temp) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 3), guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "horizontal")

h1_combined <- h1_combined + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
h1_combined

ggsave("../output/Fig1_H1_combined.tiff", h1_combined,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "cm", dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. H2 — Indirect effects through contrasting causal pathways

> **Land-use effects are mediated through contrasting causal pathways — agricultural management drives faunal activity primarily through altered microclimate, whereas treed habitats sustain faunal activity more consistently through root resource supply.**

### How the pathway estimates are computed

The SEM estimates one β per path per rolling window. An **indirect effect** is the product of all β values along a causal route. All variables are z-scored within each window, so products are standardised and comparable across windows and pathways.

| Path                    | Expression                              |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Direct                  | $\beta_9$                               |
| lu → mc₁ → fauna        | $\beta_1 \times \beta_6$                |
| lu → mc₂ → fauna        | $\beta_2 \times \beta_7$                |
| lu → root → fauna       | $\beta_5 \times \beta_8$                |
| lu → mc₁ → root → fauna | $\beta_1 \times \beta_3 \times \beta_8$ |
| lu → mc₂ → root → fauna | $\beta_2 \times \beta_4 \times \beta_8$ |
| **Total indirect**      | sum of the five routes above            |
| **Total effect**        | direct + total indirect                 |

**Sign convention:** A = 0, C = 1 — negative values indicate a stronger effect in the treed system.

### Full-dataset piecewise SEM: DAG annotation table

```{r tbl-h2-dag}
#| tbl-cap: >
#|   Path coefficients from the full-dataset piecewise SEM (all_taxa, complete
#|   time series). β = unstandardised coefficient; Std β = standardised.
#|   R²m = fixed-effects variance; R²c = fixed + random effects variance.
#|   Fisher's C tests consistency of the causal structure with the data.

fit_line <- with(dag_fit, paste0(
  "Model fit \u2014 Fisher\u2019s C = ",
  if (is.na(fishers_C)) "NA" else round(fishers_C, 2),
  " (df = ", if (is.na(df)) "NA" else df, ", p ",
  if (is.na(p_value))       "= NA (saturated model)"
  else if (p_value < 0.001) "< 0.001"
  else                       paste0("= ", round(p_value, 3)),
  ") \u2502 AIC = ",
  if (is.na(AIC)) "NA" else round(AIC, 1)
))

dag_table %>%
  filter(!str_detect(response, "~~")) %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(estimate, std_estimate, se, t_value, p_value,
             r2_marginal, r2_conditional),
           ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))),
    across(c(estimate, std_estimate, se), ~ sprintf("%.3f", .)),
    t_fmt   = if_else(is.na(t_value), "\u2014", sprintf("%.2f", t_value)),
    p_fmt   = if_else(p_value < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", p_value)),
    r2_marg = sprintf("%.3f", r2_marginal),
    r2_cond = sprintf("%.3f", r2_conditional)
  ) %>%
  select(
    "Equation"   = equation,
    "Predictor"  = predictor,
    "\u03b2"     = estimate,
    "Std \u03b2" = std_estimate,
    "SE"         = se,
    "t"          = t_fmt,
    "p"          = p_fmt,
    "sig"        = sig,
    "R\u00b2m"   = r2_marg,
    "R\u00b2c"   = r2_cond
  ) %>%
  kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE,
      align  = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r", "l", "r", "r")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("condensed", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8e8e8") %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = c(1, 9, 10), valign = "middle") %>%
  footnote(
    general = paste0(fit_line,
                     "<br>Sig. codes: *** p < 0.001 &nbsp;** p < 0.01 ",
                     "&nbsp;* p < 0.05 &nbsp;. p < 0.10"),
    general_title = "", escape = FALSE, footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

### Full-dataset vs rolling-window pathway estimates

```{r h2-prep}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PREPARE H2 TIME SERIES
#
# direct_ts   — land_use → fauna β from rolling-window SEM results.
# indirect_ts — β-products from indirect_effects, pivoted to long format.
# Both restricted to all_taxa (community level) and joined with path_meta.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

direct_ts <- sem_results %>%
  filter(taxon == "all_taxa",
         predictor == "land_use",
         response  == "fauna") %>%
  transmute(path_id = "direct", date_start, season, std_estimate)

indirect_ts <- indirect_effects %>%
  filter(taxon == "all_taxa") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    c(via_mc1, via_mc2, via_root, via_mc1_root, via_mc2_root,
      total_indirect, total_effect),
    names_to  = "path_id",
    values_to = "std_estimate"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(std_estimate)) %>%
  mutate(
    month  = month(date_start),
    season = factor(case_when(
      month %in% 3:5  ~ "Spring",
      month %in% 6:8  ~ "Summer",
      month %in% 9:11 ~ "Autumn",
      TRUE            ~ "Winter"
    ), levels = season_order)
  ) %>%
  select(path_id, date_start, season, std_estimate)

# Winsorise at 2.5–97.5th percentile per pathway before smoothing to reduce
# leverage of outlier windows without removing them from the raw data.
h2_ts <- bind_rows(direct_ts, indirect_ts) %>%
  left_join(path_meta, by = "path_id") %>%
  mutate(
    path_id = factor(path_id, levels = path_meta$path_id),
    label   = factor(label,   levels = path_meta$label),
    season  = factor(season,  levels = season_order)
  ) %>%
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  mutate(
    lo           = quantile(std_estimate, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi           = quantile(std_estimate, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    std_estimate = pmax(lo, pmin(hi, std_estimate))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


#' Fit a LOESS smoother to one pathway's time series
#'
#' @param df    Data frame with `date_start` (Date) and `std_estimate`.
#' @param span  LOESS bandwidth (default 0.30).
#' @return `df` with an added `smoothed` column.
smooth_path <- function(df, span = 0.30) {
  df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(std_estimate)) %>% arrange(date_start)
  if (nrow(df) < 5) return(mutate(df, smoothed = std_estimate))
  fit <- tryCatch(
    loess(std_estimate ~ as.numeric(date_start), data = df, span = span,
          control = loess.control(surface = "direct")),
    error = \(e) NULL
  )
  df$smoothed <- if (!is.null(fit)) predict(fit) else df$std_estimate
  df
}

h2_smoothed <- h2_ts %>%
  mutate(date_start = as.Date(date_start)) %>%
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  group_modify(~ smooth_path(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()

date_range_h2 <- range(h2_smoothed$date_start)

# ── Shared y-axis limits ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
shared_ylim_h2 <- c(
  floor(  min(c(h2_ts$std_estimate, h2_smoothed$smoothed), na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10,
  ceiling(max(c(h2_ts$std_estimate, h2_smoothed$smoothed), na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10
)

# ── Abbreviated x-axis labels for the marginal panel ─────────────────────────
# Names must be the full label strings (as stored in levels(h2_marginal$label))
# because scale_x_discrete(labels = ...) matches by break value, not path_id.
path_labels_short <- setNames(
  c("Direct", paste0("mc1\u2192fauna"), paste0("mc2\u2192fauna"),
    "root\u2192fauna", "mc1\u2192root\n\u2192fauna",
    "mc2\u2192root\n\u2192fauna", "Total\nindirect", "Total\neffect"),
  path_meta$label
)
```

```{r h2-global-coef}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# EXTRACT GLOBAL-MODEL PATHWAY ESTIMATES
#
# global_coef: one row per directional path with standardised β and SE.
# gc() / gse() are helper extractors used to build β-products.
# se_global uses a first-order product approximation (product of individual
# SEs) — conservative, suitable for display error bars only.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

global_coef <- dag_table %>%
  filter(!str_detect(response, "~~")) %>%
  mutate(
    std_estimate = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(std_estimate)),
    se           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(se))
  ) %>%
  select(response, predictor, b = std_estimate, se)

gc  <- function(resp, pred) {
  v <- global_coef$b[global_coef$response == resp & global_coef$predictor == pred]
  if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else v[1]
}
gse <- function(resp, pred) {
  v <- global_coef$se[global_coef$response == resp & global_coef$predictor == pred]
  if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else v[1]
}

lu_mc1   <- gc("microclimate_1", "land_use");  se_lu_mc1   <- gse("microclimate_1", "land_use")
lu_mc2   <- gc("microclimate_2", "land_use");  se_lu_mc2   <- gse("microclimate_2", "land_use")
mc1_root <- gc("root", "microclimate_1");      se_mc1_root <- gse("root", "microclimate_1")
mc2_root <- gc("root", "microclimate_2");      se_mc2_root <- gse("root", "microclimate_2")
lu_root  <- gc("root", "land_use");            se_lu_root  <- gse("root", "land_use")
mc1_fau  <- gc("fauna", "microclimate_1");     se_mc1_fau  <- gse("fauna", "microclimate_1")
mc2_fau  <- gc("fauna", "microclimate_2");     se_mc2_fau  <- gse("fauna", "microclimate_2")
root_fau <- gc("fauna", "root");               se_root_fau <- gse("fauna", "root")
lu_fau   <- gc("fauna", "land_use");           se_lu_fau   <- gse("fauna", "land_use")

global_paths <- tribble(
  ~path_id,         ~b_global,                               ~se_global,
  "direct",          lu_fau,                                  se_lu_fau,
  "via_mc1",         lu_mc1  * mc1_fau,                       se_lu_mc1  * se_mc1_fau,
  "via_mc2",         lu_mc2  * mc2_fau,                       se_lu_mc2  * se_mc2_fau,
  "via_root",        lu_root * root_fau,                      se_lu_root * se_root_fau,
  "via_mc1_root",    lu_mc1  * mc1_root * root_fau,           se_lu_mc1  * se_mc1_root * se_root_fau,
  "via_mc2_root",    lu_mc2  * mc2_root * root_fau,           se_lu_mc2  * se_mc2_root * se_root_fau,
  "total_indirect",  (lu_mc1 * mc1_fau) + (lu_mc2 * mc2_fau),(se_lu_mc1 * se_mc1_fau) + (se_lu_mc2 * se_mc2_fau),
  "total_effect",    lu_fau  + (lu_mc1  * mc1_fau),           se_lu_fau  + (se_lu_mc1  * se_mc1_fau)
) %>%
  left_join(path_meta, by = "path_id") %>%
  mutate(path_id = factor(path_id, levels = path_meta$path_id),
         label   = factor(label,   levels = path_meta$label))

# Rolling-window marginal summaries per pathway (median, IQR, 95% range)
h2_marginal <- h2_ts %>%
  group_by(path_id, label, colour, ltype) %>%
  summarise(
    median_b = median(std_estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_b     = sd(std_estimate,     na.rm = TRUE),
    q025     = quantile(std_estimate, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    q975     = quantile(std_estimate, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    q25      = quantile(std_estimate, 0.25,  na.rm = TRUE),
    q75      = quantile(std_estimate, 0.75,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  select(-any_of(c("b_global", "se_global"))) %>%
  left_join(select(global_paths, path_id, b_global, se_global), by = "path_id")
```

```{r fig-h2-combined}
#| label: fig-h2-combined
#| fig-cap: >
#|   **Causal pathway decomposition of land-use effects on faunal activity (H2).**
#|   (A) Marginal distribution of standardised β-products across all rolling
#|   windows per pathway. Thick bar = IQR; thin bar = 95% range; open circle =
#|   rolling-window median; open diamond (nudged right) = global model estimate
#|   ± 1 SE. (B) LOESS-smoothed (span = 0.30) time series. Solid = direct or
#|   composite; dashed = two-step indirect; dotted = three-step indirect.
#|   Shaded bands = seasons. Dotted red lines = agricultural interventions.
#| fig-width: 16
#| fig-height: 6

shape_values <- c(median = 21, global = 23)
legend_dummy <- tibble(x     = c(NA_character_, NA_character_),
                       y     = c(NA_real_,      NA_real_),
                       shape = c("median",      "global"))

# ── Panel A: marginal summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_marginal <- ggplot(h2_marginal, aes(colour = path_id)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_linerange(aes(x = label, ymin = q025, ymax = q975),
                 linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.30) +
  geom_linerange(aes(x = label, ymin = q25, ymax = q75),
                 linewidth = 2.2, alpha = 0.60) +
  geom_point(aes(x = label, y = median_b, shape = "median"),
             size = 2.4, fill = "white", stroke = 1.1) +
  geom_linerange(aes(x = label,
                     ymin = b_global - se_global,
                     ymax = b_global + se_global),
                 position  = position_nudge(x = 0.28),
                 linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.35, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(aes(x = label, y = b_global, shape = "global"),
             position = position_nudge(x = 0.28),
             size = 2.2, fill = "white", stroke = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(data    = legend_dummy,
             mapping = aes(x = x, y = y, shape = shape),
             colour = "grey30", fill = "white",
             size = 2.2, stroke = 1.0, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name   = NULL,
    values = shape_values,
    labels = c(median = "Rolling median (IQR / 95%)",
               global = "Global model (\u00b1 1 SE)"),
    guide  = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(fill   = c("white", "white"),
                          colour = "grey30",
                          size   = c(2.4, 2.2))
    )
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = levels(h2_marginal$label),
                   labels  = path_labels_short) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim_h2) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Standardised \u03b2") +
  theme_sem(legend_pos = "bottom") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 5.5, angle = 40, hjust = 1,
                                      lineheight = 0.85),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 6.5),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 6.5),
    legend.key.size    = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.border       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y.left   = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.margin        = margin(t = 5, r = 0, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Panel B: LOESS temporal time series ───────────────────────────────────────
p_time <- ggplot(
  h2_smoothed %>% mutate(date_start = as.Date(date_start)),
  aes(x = date_start, y = smoothed,
      colour = path_id, linetype = path_id)
) +
  make_season_layers(date_range_h2, y_label_pos = shared_ylim_h2[2] * 0.9) +
  geom_vline(data = interventions, aes(xintercept = date),
             colour = "darkred", linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.65) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours, labels = path_labels,
                      name = "Pathway") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = path_ltypes, labels = path_labels,
                        name = "Pathway") +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "3 months",
               expand = expansion(mult = 0.01)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim_h2) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_sem(legend_pos = "bottom") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x        = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 6.5),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 6.0),
    legend.title       = element_text(size = 6.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.width   = unit(16, "pt"),
    legend.key.height  = unit(10, "pt"),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y        = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y       = element_blank(),
    panel.border       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y.right  = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.margin        = margin(t = 5, r = 5, b = 5, l = 0, unit = "pt")
  )

# ── Assemble ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
h2_combined <- (p_marginal + p_time) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 3), guides = "keep")
h2_combined <- h2_combined + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
h2_combined

ggsave("../output/Fig2_H2_combined.tiff", h2_combined,
       width = 24, height = 12, units = "cm", dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. H3 — Taxonomic response diversity under contrasting land-use forcing

> **Does land-use intensification compress taxonomic response diversity? Understrong land-use effects, do individual taxa converge toward similar responses, or does winner-loser dynamics amplify inter-taxon heterogeneity?**

### Conceptual basis: the coefficient of variation as a response-diversity metric

For each rolling window and each causal pathway the SEM estimates one standardised β per taxon — the strength of the land-use effect on that taxon's activity through that pathway. The **per-window coefficient of variation** (CV) summarises the relative dispersion of these individual responses:

$$\text{CV}_w = \frac{\text{SD}(\beta_{\text{taxa},w})}{\overline{|\beta_{\text{taxa},w}|} + \varepsilon}$$

A high CV means taxa respond very differently from each other (high response diversity); a low CV means they respond similarly (convergence). Dividing by the mean absolute β removes the effect-size confound: even when all taxa shift together as the community signal strengthens, a constant CV indicates proportional rather than convergent responses. A **declining CV** as the community β increases is the signature of genuine convergence.

The predictor on the x-axis is the **signed, pathway-matched community β**: positive values correspond to strong land-use effect windows.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Parameters

```{r h3-params}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ANALYSIS PARAMETERS
#
# pathway_focus   : pathways included in the CV analysis. total_effect excluded
#                   — it compounds direct + indirect and would be redundant
#                   given that both are already shown.
# eps             : small constant added to the CV denominator to avoid
#                   division by zero in windows where all taxon β ≈ 0.
# pathway_order   : panel display order (causal depth: direct → two-step
#                   → three-step → composite).
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

pathway_focus <- c(
  "direct",                         # β₉            — land_use → fauna
  "via_mc1",    "via_mc2",          # β₁×β₆, β₂×β₇ — two-step microclimate
  "via_root",                       # β₅×β₈         — two-step root
  "via_mc1_root", "via_mc2_root",   # three-step chains through microclimate → root
  "total_indirect"                  # sum of all five indirect routes
)

eps          <- 1e-4

pathway_order <- pathway_focus
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Data preparation

```{r h3-data}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BUILD PER-WINDOW, PER-TAXON LONG TABLE
#
# h3_direct_win   — direct land_use → fauna β (from sem_results).
# h3_indirect_win — indirect β-products (from indirect_effects, pivoted long).
#
# comm_beta_win   — community (all_taxa) β per pathway × window.
#                   Pathway-matched: the CV for via_root is plotted against the
#                   all_taxa via_root β, not the direct β. This ensures the
#                   x-axis forcing proxy is ecologically meaningful for each
#                   panel (via_root forcing → via_root response diversity).
#
# h3_taxa_win     — individual taxa only (all_taxa excluded from CV computation).
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

h3_direct_win <- sem_results %>%
  filter(predictor == "land_use", response == "fauna") %>%
  transmute(taxon, date_start = as.Date(date_start),
            path_id = "direct", std_estimate)

h3_indirect_win <- indirect_effects %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(via_mc1, via_mc2, via_root,
                  via_mc1_root, via_mc2_root,
                  total_indirect, total_effect),
    names_to  = "path_id", values_to = "std_estimate"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(std_estimate), path_id %in% pathway_focus) %>%
  mutate(date_start = as.Date(date_start)) %>%
  select(taxon, path_id, date_start, std_estimate)

# Season derived from date_start for pipeline-version independence
h3_win_raw <- bind_rows(h3_direct_win, h3_indirect_win) %>%
  filter(path_id %in% pathway_focus) 

# Pathway-matched community β (signed) — one row per path_id × date_start
comm_beta_win <- h3_win_raw %>%
  filter(taxon == "all_taxa") %>%
  select(path_id, date_start, community_beta = std_estimate)

h3_taxa_win <- h3_win_raw %>%
  filter(taxon != "all_taxa")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### CV computation

```{r h3-cv}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMPUTE PER-WINDOW COEFFICIENT OF VARIATION
#
# For each (path_id × date_start) group, the CV is:
#
#   CV = SD(β_taxa) / (mean|β_taxa| + eps)
#
# This is a relative dispersion measure: it answers "how spread out are the
# individual taxon responses *relative to the typical effect size*?". Because
# the denominator absorbs the overall effect magnitude, CV is immune to the
# mathematical artifact that inflates raw distances in high-forcing windows.
#
# The resulting h3_cv table has one row per (path_id × date_start), which
# eliminates within-window pseudoreplication entirely — no random effect for
# pairs is needed in the downstream GLS.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

#' Compute the per-window CV for one (pathway × window) slice
#'
#' @param df  Slice with columns `taxon` and `std_estimate`. 
#' @return    Single-row tibble with cv and n_taxa, or empty tibble.
compute_cv <- function(df) {
  taxa <- unique(df$taxon)
  beta_vec <- df$std_estimate
  tibble(
    cv     = sd(beta_vec, na.rm = TRUE) / (mean(abs(beta_vec), na.rm = TRUE) + eps),
    n_taxa = length(taxa)
  )
}

h3_cv <- h3_taxa_win %>%
  group_by(path_id, date_start) %>%
  group_modify(~ compute_cv(.x)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Attach pathway-matched, signed community β
  left_join(comm_beta_win, by = c("path_id", "date_start")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cv), !is.na(community_beta)) %>%
  mutate(path_id = factor(path_id, levels = pathway_order))%>%
  mutate(community_beta = abs(community_beta))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### GLS model fitting

```{r h3-gls}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FIT GLS: cv ~ community_beta + AR(1)
#
# Because h3_cv contains one observation per window (no pair-level
# pseudoreplication), a simple GLS with an AR(1) residual structure is the
# appropriate model. No random effects are needed.
#
# Predictor: signed community_beta (pathway-matched).
#   Negative slope → CV decreases as cultivated forcing strengthens → convergence.
#   Positive slope → CV increases → winner-loser divergence.
#
# AR(1) within the window time series absorbs the heavy overlap between
# adjacent rolling windows (daily steps, 14-day span ≈ 13/14 overlap).
#
# ML estimation for valid fixed-effect p-values.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

#' Fit one GLS(cv ~ community_beta, AR(1)) for a single pathway slice
#'
#' @param df  Pathway-specific slice of h3_cv with columns cv, community_beta,
#'            and date_start.
#' @return    A gls object, or NULL on hard failure.
fit_cv_gls <- function(df) {
  df <- df %>% mutate(date_num = as.numeric(date_start))
  fit_once <- function(d)
    nlme::gls(
      cv ~ community_beta,
      correlation = nlme::corAR1(form = ~ date_num),
      method      = "ML",
      data        = d,
      control     = nlme::glsControl(returnObject = TRUE)
    )
  tryCatch(
    fit_once(df),
    warning = function(w) suppressWarnings(fit_once(df)),
    error   = function(e) { message("GLS failed: ", e$message); NULL }
  )
}

# ── Fit per pathway ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cv_fits <- h3_cv %>%
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  group_modify(~ tibble(fit = list(fit_cv_gls(.x)), n_obs = nrow(.x))) %>%
  ungroup()

# ── Extract fixed-effect statistics ───────────────────────────────────────────
# R²m for GLS: var(fitted) / (var(fitted) + var(residuals))
# This is the fraction of total variance explained by community_beta alone.
cv_stats <- cv_fits %>%
  filter(!map_lgl(fit, is.null)) %>%
  mutate(
    tbl      = map(fit, ~ summary(.x)$tTable),
    slope    = map_dbl(tbl, ~ .x["community_beta", "Value"]),
    se_slope = map_dbl(tbl, ~ .x["community_beta", "Std.Error"]),
    t_val    = map_dbl(tbl, ~ .x["community_beta", "t-value"]),
    pval     = map_dbl(tbl, ~ .x["community_beta", "p-value"]),
    r2       = map_dbl(fit, function(m) {
      vf <- var(fitted(m)); vr <- var(residuals(m))
      vf / (vf + vr)
    }),
    p_fmt = case_when(pval < 0.001 ~ "p < 0.001",
                      TRUE         ~ sprintf("p = %.3f", pval)),
    sig   = case_when(pval < 0.001 ~ "***", pval < 0.01 ~ "**",
                      pval < 0.05  ~ "*",   pval < 0.10 ~ ".",
                      TRUE         ~ "ns"),
    # Ecological interpretation: negative slope = convergence under forcing
    result = case_when(
      slope < 0 & pval < 0.05 ~ "\u2191 convergence",
      slope > 0 & pval < 0.05 ~ "\u2193 divergence",
      TRUE                     ~ "ns"
    ),
    # Multi-line annotation for figure panels
    label = sprintf("\u03b2 = %+.3f\nR\u00b2m = %.3f\n%s %s",
                    slope, r2, p_fmt, sig)
  ) %>%
  select(-tbl)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Main figure: CV vs. community β

```{r fig-h3-cv}
#| label: fig-h3-cv
#| fig-cap: >
#|   **Per-window coefficient of variation (CV) of individual taxon land-use
#|   responses vs signed community β across all causal pathways (H3).** One
#|   point per rolling window; colour encodes pathway (same palette as Fig. 2). x-axis: signed, pathway-matched community β
#|   (positive = cultivated-dominant; negative = treed-dominant). y-axis:
#|   CV = SD(β_taxa) / mean|β_taxa|, a scale-free measure of response
#|   heterogeneity among individual taxa. A negative slope (dark line) supports
#|   convergence under cultivated forcing; a positive slope indicates
#|   winner-loser divergence. Line = GLS fixed-effect slope with 95% CI.
#|   Panel annotations: β (slope), R²m (marginal R²), and p (GLS t-test).
#| fig-width: 15
#| fig-height: 9

pathway_labels_h3 <- setNames(path_meta$label, path_meta$path_id)

p_cv <- h3_cv %>%
  mutate(path_id = factor(path_id, levels = pathway_order)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = community_beta, y = cv)) +

  facet_wrap(~ path_id, nrow = 2, scales = "free",
             labeller = as_labeller(pathway_labels_h3)) +

  # Zero reference on x (boundary between cultivated- and treed-dominant)
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3,
             linetype = "dashed") +

  # Window observations — colour by pathway
  geom_point(aes(colour = path_id),
             alpha = 0.55, size = 1.2) +

  # GLS fixed-effect slope (OLS used for display; slope identical for balanced data)
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
              colour = "grey15", fill = "grey40",
              linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.15) +

  # Per-panel annotation anchored to top-right corner regardless of free scale
  geom_text(data        = cv_stats,
            mapping     = aes(label = label),
            x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.08, vjust = 1.25,
            size = 2.0, colour = "grey20", lineheight = 1.3,
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name   = "Community \u03b2 (pathway-matched, signed)",
    labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
  labs(
    y        = "CV of taxon \u03b2s  [SD / mean|\u03b2|]",
    title    = "H3 \u2014 Taxonomic response diversity under contrasting land-use forcing",
    subtitle = "Negative slope = convergence under cultivated forcing | Positive = winner-loser divergence"
  ) +
  guides(colour = "none") +
  theme_sem(base_size = 8, legend_pos = "right") +
  theme(
    strip.text  = element_text(size = 6.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text   = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6.5)
  )

p_cv

ggsave("../output/Fig3_H3_cv.tiff", p_cv,
       width = 24, height = 14, units = "cm", dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### GLS summary table

```{r tbl-h3-cv}
#| tbl-cap: >
#|   GLS results for CV ~ community β across all causal pathways. β = slope on
#|   the signed, pathway-matched community β; a negative β supports convergence
#|   (CV decreases as cultivated forcing strengthens). R²m = marginal R²
#|   (variance explained by the fixed effect alone). p = parametric p-value
#|   from the GLS t-test, accounting for AR(1) residual autocorrelation.

cv_stats %>%
  left_join(select(path_meta, path_id), by = "path_id") %>%
  mutate(
    slope_fmt = sprintf("%+.4f", slope),
    se_fmt    = sprintf("%.4f",  se_slope),
    r2_fmt    = sprintf("%.4f",  r2),
    n_fmt     = formatC(n_obs, format = "d", big.mark = ",")
  ) %>%
  arrange(match(path_id, pathway_order)) %>%
  select(
    "Pathway"              = label,
    "\u03b2"               = slope_fmt,
    "SE"                   = se_fmt,
    "R\u00b2m"             = r2_fmt,
    "p (GLS)"              = p_fmt,
    "Sig."                 = sig,
    "Result"               = result,
    "N (windows)"          = n_fmt
  ) %>%
  kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE,
      align  = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "l", "l", "r")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("condensed", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8e8e8") %>%
  footnote(
    general = paste0(
      "GLS: CV ~ community \u03b2 (signed, pathway-matched) + AR(1). ",
      "ML estimation (nlme::gls). ",
      "CV = SD(\u03b2_taxa) / (mean|\u03b2_taxa| + \u03b5), \u03b5 = 1\u00d710\u207b\u2074. ",
      "Negative \u03b2 = convergence (CV decreases with cultivated forcing). ",
      "Sig. codes: *** p < 0.001 \u00a0** p < 0.01 \u00a0* p < 0.05 \u00a0. p < 0.10."
    ),
    general_title = "", escape = FALSE, footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### GLS diagnostics

```{r fig-h3-diagnostics}
#| label: fig-h3-diagnostics
#| fig-cap: >
#|   **GLS residual diagnostics for CV ~ community β per pathway.**
#|   (A) Residuals vs fitted values: random scatter around zero indicates
#|   linearity and homoscedasticity; a systematic curve or fan flags problems.
#|   (B) Normal Q-Q of standardised residuals: points should track the
#|   diagonal; heavy tails indicate non-normality. (C) Scale-location
#|   (√|std. residuals| vs fitted): a flat LOESS confirms homoscedasticity;
#|   a rising trend indicates variance increasing with the mean. (D) ACF of
#|   GLS residuals: bars should fall within the confidence ribbon at lag > 1,
#|   confirming that the AR(1) structure adequately absorbed temporal
#|   autocorrelation. Persistent bars at lag 2–3 would suggest a higher AR
#|   order or seasonal structure not captured by the model.
#| fig-width: 16
#| fig-height: 16

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# GLS DIAGNOSTICS
#
# For each fitted GLS object in cv_fits, four diagnostics are computed:
#
#   resid_std   — residuals standardised by their SD (response residuals / SD)
#   fitted_val  — GLS fitted values
#   sqrt_abs    — √|resid_std| for the scale-location plot
#   acf         — sample ACF of raw residuals up to lag 20
#
# The confidence interval for the ACF is the standard ±1.96/√n band under the
# white-noise null hypothesis.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── Observation-level diagnostics ─────────────────────────────────────────────
diag_obs <- cv_fits %>%
  filter(!map_lgl(fit, is.null)) %>%
  mutate(
    diag = map2(fit, path_id, function(m, pid) {
      res <- residuals(m, type = "response")
      std <- res / sd(res)
      tibble(
        path_id   = as.character(pid),
        fitted    = fitted(m),
        resid_std = std,
        sqrt_abs  = sqrt(abs(std))
      )
    })
  ) %>%
  select(diag) %>%
  unnest(diag) %>%
  mutate(path_id = factor(path_id, levels = pathway_order))

# ── ACF diagnostics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
diag_acf <- cv_fits %>%
  filter(!map_lgl(fit, is.null)) %>%
  mutate(
    acf_out = map2(fit, path_id, function(m, pid) {
      a  <- acf(residuals(m), plot = FALSE, lag.max = 20)
      ci <- qnorm(0.975) / sqrt(length(residuals(m)))
      tibble(
        path_id = as.character(pid),
        lag     = as.integer(a$lag[-1]),
        acf     = as.numeric(a$acf[-1]),
        ci      = ci
      )
    })
  ) %>%
  select(acf_out) %>%
  unnest(acf_out) %>%
  mutate(path_id = factor(path_id, levels = pathway_order))


# ── Panel A: Residuals vs Fitted ──────────────────────────────────────────────
p_da <- ggplot(diag_obs, aes(x = fitted, y = resid_std, colour = path_id)) +
  facet_wrap(~ path_id, nrow = 2, scales = "free",
             labeller = as_labeller(pathway_labels_h3)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey55", linetype = "dashed",
             linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.30, size = 0.7, shape = 16) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE,
              colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours) +
  labs(x = "Fitted values", y = "Std. residuals",
       title = "A \u2014 Residuals vs Fitted  [linearity & homoscedasticity]") +
  guides(colour = "none") +
  theme_sem(base_size = 7) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 5.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 7.5))

# ── Panel B: Normal Q-Q ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_db <- ggplot(diag_obs, aes(sample = resid_std, colour = path_id)) +
  facet_wrap(~ path_id, nrow = 2, scales = "free",
             labeller = as_labeller(pathway_labels_h3)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.30, size = 0.7, shape = 16) +
  stat_qq_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.55, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours) +
  labs(x = "Theoretical quantiles", y = "Std. residuals",
       title = "B \u2014 Normal Q-Q  [normality of residuals]") +
  guides(colour = "none") +
  theme_sem(base_size = 7) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 5.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 7.5))

# ── Panel C: Scale-Location ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_dc <- ggplot(diag_obs, aes(x = fitted, y = sqrt_abs, colour = path_id)) +
  facet_wrap(~ path_id, nrow = 2, scales = "free",
             labeller = as_labeller(pathway_labels_h3)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.30, size = 0.7, shape = 16) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE,
              colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours) +
  labs(x = "Fitted values", y = expression(sqrt("|Std. residuals|")),
       title = "C \u2014 Scale-Location  [homoscedasticity]") +
  guides(colour = "none") +
  theme_sem(base_size = 7) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 5.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 7.5))

# ── Panel D: ACF of residuals ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_dd <- ggplot(diag_acf, aes(x = lag, y = acf, colour = path_id,
                              fill = path_id)) +
  facet_wrap(~ path_id, nrow = 2, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = as_labeller(pathway_labels_h3)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3, colour = "grey40") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = -ci, ymax = ci), alpha = 0.12, colour = NA) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = lag, yend = 0), linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 1.1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = path_colours) +
  scale_fill_manual(values   = path_colours) +
  labs(x = "Lag", y = "ACF",
       title = "D \u2014 ACF of GLS residuals  [residual autocorrelation after AR(1)]") +
  guides(colour = "none", fill = "none") +
  theme_sem(base_size = 7) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 5.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 7.5))

# ── Assemble 4-panel grid ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
(p_da / p_db / p_dc / p_dd) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "GLS diagnostics \u2014 CV ~ community \u03b2 per pathway",
    subtitle = paste(
      "A/C: non-flat LOESS \u2192 heteroscedasticity.",
      "B: heavy Q-Q tails \u2192 non-normal residuals.",
      "D: bars outside ribbon at lag > 1 \u2192 residual autocorrelation.",
      sep = "  |  "
    ),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 6.5, colour = "grey40")
    )
  )
```
